old_claim.note: Maritime Insurance provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Spanish galleons supplied the convoy's heavily armed ocean-going cargo-and-warship platform, combining treasure transport with defensive firepower.

source_shape.source_locator: The article says Spanish galleons and other ships sailed in the fleets, galleons were heavily armed, and in convoy one galleon could give protective fire for another.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source presents galleons as the armed oceangoing ships that carried treasure and protected each other in convoy.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Galleon ship design is therefore an enabling platform for the scoped treasure-fleet convoys. Maritime insurance may price risk, but it is not evidenced as a direct prerequisite for Spain's armed convoy requirement.

ontology_before: Naval Convoy Systems depended on Maritime Insurance, implying insurance rules enabled the protected-fleet system.
ontology_after: Spanish Treasure Fleet Convoys depend on galleon_ship_design as the armed cargo-and-warship platform for convoy operation.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: naval_convoy_systems now depends on galleon_ship_design and no longer directly depends on maritime_insurance.
would_reject_if: The node were broadened back to maritime insurance institutions rather than Spanish armed treasure-fleet convoys.
A source showed marine insurance was required for the Spanish Crown to mandate convoy sailing.
The galleon_ship_design node were later narrowed to exclude armed Spanish treasure-fleet ships.
short_reason: The source-backed platform is the armed galleon, not maritime insurance.
